decisions.title: Finalize gRPC API for Public Release
decisions.context: Multiple signals (ENG-9401, ENG-9070, ENG-9400) indicate gRPC API work nearing completion. Policy API needs validation endpoint (ENG-9398) before user release. This is critical for 'Build the Moat' pillar - enabling programmatic access deepens integration.

arguments.for: Public API enables deeper customer integrations, supports 'Embed Deeper' pillar, competitive moat feature. Documentation generation already tracked (ENG-9400).
arguments.against: Rushing API release without proper validation could create breaking changes later. Need to ensure performance is acceptable (ENG-8958, ENG-8957 latency issues exist).

decisions.title: Address Data Ingestion Memory Growth Issue
decisions.context: ENG-9391 flags unbounded memory growth in ingest service read-buffer. This affects system reliability and 'Nail the Core' pillar. Critical infrastructure issue that could impact all customers.

arguments.for: Core reliability issue affecting all data ingestion. Memory leaks can cause cascading failures. Immediate priority to maintain trust.
arguments.against: May require significant architectural changes. Could delay feature work if we pull engineers from Explore V2 or Canvas efforts.

decisions.title: Prioritize Family Stats in E2 vs. Cold Storage Spikes
decisions.context: Active work on family stats (ENG-7858, DES-115, ENG-9394) conflicts with cold storage exploration spikes (ENG-9395, ENG-9396). Both relate to Canvas/Explore V2. Need resource allocation decision.

arguments.for: Family stats directly improve user workflows for rules/annotations (Signal>Evidence>Decision). Completing E2 features increases 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' metric.
arguments.against: Cold storage is infrastructure for scale ('Build the Moat'). Delaying spikes could bottleneck future Canvas capabilities and prevent handling larger datasets.

decisions.title: Consolidate UX Polish Tasks into Single Sprint
decisions.context: Multiple UX issues (ENG-9373, DES-55, ENG-9106, DES-150, ENG-9392, ENG-9378) scattered across products. Fragmented UX work reduces 'Nail the Core' effectiveness.

arguments.for: Batching UX fixes improves user experience coherently, supports 'Nail the Core'. Reduces context switching for designers and frontend engineers.
arguments.against: Some UX issues may be blocking specific customer workflows. Delaying could impact satisfaction. Coordination overhead across multiple product areas.
